consent_matrix.required_consent: scope/mode itself is the explicit choice (no separate flag)

explicit flag; **`mode == full-stack` alone is NOT enough** â€” full-stack additionally requires `--with-service` **and** `--with-proxy` together, or the plan stays `BLOCKED` (`blocked_reasons: ["full_stack_confirmation"]`, #293 fix: mode name is never itself treated as consent)

mutations.effect: creates/updates the runtime's entry file (`AGENTS.md`, `GEMINI.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `.kiro/steering/simplicio-loop.md`, `CONVENTIONS.md`) between `<!-- simplicio-loop:begin/end -->` markers
mutations.reversible: yes (marker-delimited block is removable without touching the rest of the file)
yes (JSON merge; existing unrelated keys untouched)

mutations.consent_required: no â€” but a *foreign* existing hook is never overwritten (logged, not clobbered)
mutations.effect: writes `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (only if the target is a git repo and no foreign hook already lives there)

mutations.consent_required: **yes for `--break-system-packages`** â€” only attempted when pip's stderr specifically reports `externally-managed-environment` AND `--allow-break-system-packages` (or `SIMPLICIO_ALLOW_BREAK_SYSTEM_PACKAGES=1`) was explicitly passed (#293 Â§3 hardening, see below)
mutations.reversible: **not reversible by this installer** (a real `pip uninstall` is a separate, manual step)
mutations.scope: **global** Python environment (whatever `sys.executable` resolves to â€” a venv if active, else the system/user Python)

mutations.effect: symlinks console-scripts (`simplicio-dev-cli`, `simplicio-mapper`, `simplicio-loop`) into `~/.local/bin` when a `--user` pip install dropped them off PATH

mutations.consent_required: **yes** â€” only runs when `--with-service`/`--full-stack` consent is given (#293 fix: no longer runs by default)
mutations.effect: `pip install -U .[onnx]` / `simplicio-loop[onnx]` + tray dep (`pystray`+`pillow` or `rumps` on macOS)

mutations.consent_required: **yes** â€” only in `--mode full-stack` with explicit `--with-service`/`--with-proxy`
mutations.effect: copies `engine/` (capture-proxy code) and `app/` (tray code) into the target â€” the file surface of `full-stack` mode

mutations.consent_required: **yes** â€” requires `--with-service` (or `--full-stack`); OFF by default (#293 fix: was previously default-on, gated only by opt-out `--minimal`)
mutations.effect: registers the always-on capture proxy (`install_services.py install`/`wire` on Linux/Windows, `setup_simplicio.sh` â€” launchd â€” on macOS) + opens the Token Monitor dashboard once
mutations.reversible: services: yes (`install_services.py uninstall`); dashboard open: not applicable (no persistent mutation)

mutations.effect: Linux: writes a `systemd --user` unit; Windows: registers a Startup-folder shim; wiring: edits provider base-URL env for Claude/Codex/Simplicio Agent

wraps every one of the above FILE effects (skills/hooks/scripts/entry/claude_settings, + `engine`/`app` in full-stack mode) with a pre-mutation backup + before/after hash + persisted receipt under `<target>/.simplicio/receipts/<id>.json`; automatic rollback of every already-applied step if a later step raises

mutations.consent_required: no (this only ever removes paths the transaction's OWN prior manifest claims responsibility for)
mutations.effect: removes a skill directory that a PRIOR install's manifest recorded but the CURRENT release no longer declares (an N-1 â†’ N upgrade cleanup)
mutations.reversible: yes (same backup/restore mechanism as every other step)

os_differences.windows: `os.symlink` requires Developer Mode or admin â€” falls back silently (best-effort; `_link_console_script` swallows `OSError`)

`os.chmod` is a no-op on most Windows filesystems; git only needs the file to be invocable via `sh` (`#!/usr/bin/env sh` shebang), which the file already has â€” the failed `chmod` is caught and logged as non-fatal
rare (python.org/Store installs are not externally-managed) â€” the detection in `_is_externally_managed_error()` still applies uniformly; it simply never fires here
`os.path` mixed; hashing walks (`_hash_path`) normalize relative paths to `/` before hashing so a receipt/manifest hash is stable across OSes for the same tree content
`.exe` (pip) or `.CMD`/`.EXE` shims â€” `install_smoke.py`'s clean-room check and `_link_console_script()` both branch on `os.name == "nt"`
